California
Fair Political
Practices Cornmission
September 7′  ■990
Christian G. How■
and
Borton′  Petrini & Conron
Westchester Corporate P■
aza
■800 - 30th Street′
 4th F■oor
P.0. Box 2026
Bakersfie■d′  CA
Dear Mr. Howland:
This is in response to your request for written confirmation
of prior telephone advice regarding the relationship between a
proposed contribution of office space and the contribution provi-
iio^ns of the Political Reform Act (the uAct").1 Since your ques-
tion is general in nature, we are treating it as a request for
informal assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329(c) (copy
enclosed) . 2
OUESTION
Does a contribution of office space from a private nonprofit
organization which is part of a mutual benefit corporation to that
mutual benefit corporation violate any of the provisions of the
Act?
CONCLUS TON
A contribution of office space by a nonprofit orqanization to
a mutual benefit corporation does not vj-oLate any provision of the
Act so long as there is no connection between the donation and any
political 
candidate or carnpai.gn.
93303
Re:
Your Request for lnfOrmal Ass■
stance
Our Fi■e No. I-90-443
1' 
covernment code sections 81ooo-91015. A11 statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations. Section
18ooo, et seq. A11 references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the california code of Regulations.
2 rnfornal assistance does not
innunity provided bY an oPinion
83114; Regulation 18329(c) (3).)

or formal written advice. (Section
428 J Street, Suite 8OO . P.O. Box 8O7 o Sacramento CA 95804´0807 ● (916)322‐5660


File No. T-9o-443
Page 2
FACTS
You represent a nonprofit, mutual benefit corporation (the
Itcorporationrt) , lrhich includes public as well as private entities
in its mernbership. A private member wishes to donate office space
to the corporation; the corporation's costs for this space erould
be linited to its operating expenses. A concern has been raised
that the proposed donation rnight constitute an i1lega1 political
contribution.
A telephone calL to the Technical Assj,stance and Analysis
Division informed you that the contemplated arrangement regardingt
office space would not violate any provision of the Act. 
The
basis for that advice is your representation that the anticipated
donation of office space has no relationship whatsoever to any
political 
candidate or campaign. Your letter to us requests writ-
ten confirmati-on of the advice previously given to you by
telephone.
ANALYSI S
section 82015 defines |tcontributionrr as follows (in pertinent
part):
ttContributiontr means a palnnent, a forgiveness
of a 1oan, a paynent of a loan by a third party, or
an enforceable promise to make a paynent except to
the extent that fu1l and adequate consideration is
received unless it is clear fronr the surrounding
circunstances that it is not made for pol-itical
pgEpgSCS. (Enphasis added. )
Regulation 18215, copy enclosed, reiterates that a contribu-
tion is-any monetary or nonmonetary payment made for potitical
purposes. where there is no connection of any kind between a
-aonition and a candidate,3 a baltot measure committee, a
controlled conmittee, or any organization formed or existing for
political 
purposes, then there would be no contribution within the
meaning of the Act.
Your letter makes no nention of any connection that would
lead to a conclusion that the proposed donation of office space is
made for political purposes. You assert that there is rrno
political 
candidate or carnpaign involved in the situation." 
If
additionally there is no connection between the donor of the of-
fice space lnd any ballot measure conmittee, any controll-ed com-
nittee, or any organization formed or existing for political
3 A= d"firred in the Act, the
includes elected officers who
committees. (Section 82007. )
term rrcandidatett is very broad and
have not terninated their controlled


File No. I-90-443
Page 3
purposes, then there is no contribution subject to the restric-
tions of the Act.
I trust the above answers your question. If you need further
infornation, please do not hesitate to call roe at (916) 322-590]-.
S incerely,
SH: SLB: PIh
Enclosures
